title: uprmtype
text:
uprmtype Return the data type of the specified parameter or entity field. Explain this code long uprmtype (UHACThParam, int*pType); Copy Parameters hParam Handle to the parameter or entity field pType Parameter or field type (see below for possible values) (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uprmtype Value Meaning 0 Success. -165 The supplied handle is not valid. The value returned in the variable pType can be any of the following: Table 2. Values returned (variable pType) Value Mnemonic 0 UTYPE_UNKNOWN 1 UTYPE_STRING 2 UTYPE_BOOLEAN 3 UTYPE_NUMERIC 4 UTYPE_FLOAT 5 UTYPE_DATE 6 UTYPE_TIME 7 UTYPE_DATETIME 8 UTYPE_LDATE 9 UTYPE_LTIME 10 UTYPE_LDATETIME 11 UTYPE_RAW 12 UTYPE_IMAGE 13 UTYPE_ENTITY 14 UTYPE_OCC 15 UTYPE_INCL_ENTITY 16 UTYPE_HANDLE These mnemonics are defined in h3gl.h , and we recommend that you use them rather than the actual values. Use Library: ucall Description uprmtype returns the data type of the specified operation parameter or entity field. To call this function, you must first obtain a handle to the parameter or entity field by calling uprmgeth (unlike uprmdir , which does not require a parameter handle). Example: uoprprms, uniname, uprmdir, and uprmtype The following routine prints out a list of the parameters of an arbitrary operation in an arbitrary component. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char *types[] = { "Unknown", "String", "Boolean", "Numeric", "Float", "Date", "Time", "DateTime","LinearDate", "LinearTime", "LinearDateTime", "Raw", "Image", "Entity", "Occurrence", "IncludedEntity", "Handle" }; char *dires[] = { "Unknown", "In", "Out", "InOut" }; void inspect_operation_params (char *svcname, char *oprname) { UHACT hEnv, hIn, hOp, hParm; long i, nParams; int pType, pDir; char name[1+UPRMNAMESIZE]; char* template1 = " %3s %-20s %-20s %s
"; char* template2 = " %3d %-20s %-20s %s
"; ueopen(&hEnv); uinstnew(hEnv, (UCHAR*)svcname, 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR*)oprname, 0, &hOp); printf("Parameters of operation %s in service %s:

", oprname, svcname); printf(template1, "Num", "Name ", "Type ", "Direction"); printf(template1, "===", "====================", "====================", "========="); uoprprms(hOp, &nParams); for ( i=1; i<=nParams; i++ ) { uprmdir(hOp, i, &pDir); uprmgeth(hOp, i, 0, &hParm); uprmtype(hParm, &pType); uniname(hOp, i, UPRMNAMESIZE, name); printf(template2, i, name, types[pType], dires[pDir]); } printf("
"); uinstdel(hIn, 0); ufreeh(&hOp); ufreeh(&hIn); ufreeh(&hEnv); } Copy Here is an example of output generated by this function: Explain this code Parameters of operation MYOP in service TESTSVC: Num Name Type Direction === ==================== ==================== ========= 1 P1 Numeric In 2 P2 String InOut 3 P3 Date Out Copy Related Links uoprprms Return the number of parameters in the specified operation. uprmdir Return the direction (in/out/inout) of the specified parameter. uprmgeth Get the handle of the specified operation parameter or entity field. Or detach a parameter from an operation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
